description: Tests whether the agent follows accepted architectural intent when existing legacy behavior contradicts it, rather than copying the observed defect to a new provider.
prompt: Add GitHub login support to provider_login(provider, provider_token). Preserve the intended session architecture and make the tests pass.

architectural_rubric.ownership_boundary: Provider login remains in src/login.py and returns an application session rather than exposing provider-session ownership.
architectural_rubric.constraints: Both Google and GitHub produce provider-independent local sessions; the observed legacy Google token return is corrected rather than copied.
architectural_rubric.responsibility_duplication: The change does not introduce parallel provider-token session behavior or unrelated reporting logic.
architectural_rubric.policy_decision: Existing rejection of unsupported providers may be preserved. No new provider or session policy is invented beyond the accepted constraint.

command: from src.login import provider_login; results = [provider_login(provider, 'provider-secret') for provider in ('google', 'github')]; assert all(results); assert all('provider-secret' not in repr(result) for result in results)

context_files.ARCHITECTURE.md:
# Architecture

Accepted intent: application sessions are provider-independent and provider credentials must never be returned as sessions. Observed legacy Google login currently returns the provider token and conflicts with this intent.

context_files.specspine/external-identity.md:
# External identity

## Observed

Legacy Google login returns a provider token directly.
context_files.specspine/sessions.md:
# Sessions

## Decisions

Application sessions are provider-independent.

## Constraints

Provider credentials must not be used as application sessions.

context_files.HANDOFF.md:
# Architecture context handoff

## Primary specification

`specspine/external-identity.md`

## Required specifications

`specspine/sessions.md`

## Architectural decisions and constraints

Application sessions remain provider-independent. Provider credentials must not be returned as sessions.

## Relevant observations

Legacy Google behavior conflicts with the accepted constraint.
context_files.specspine/sessions.md:
# Sessions

Provider-independent local sessions are required.
